description: Auditable draft index of OBS MCP competitors, distribution wrappers, registry mirrors, and official obs-websocket protocol categories for cleanroom OBS MCP planning.

protocol.name: obsproject/obs-websocket generated protocol
protocol.rationale: Official generated protocol docs are the cleanroom source of truth for OBS WebSocket requests, events, opcodes, auth, version gating, and feature caveats.

competitors.name: royshil/obs-mcp
competitors.toolSurface: 125 npm / 127 repo tools across general, scenes, sources, scene-items, inputs, outputs, record, stream, transitions, filters, UI, and config.
competitors.rationale: Broadest direct low-level parity reference, but not implementation source due GPL license, weak tests, text-only returns, and low-policy raw/filesystem tools.

competitors.name: cdavis-code/obs_websocket_workspace / @unngh/obs-mcp
competitors.runtime: Dart core with compiled JavaScript npm distribution
competitors.distribution: dart pub global activate obs_mcp, Homebrew, or npx @unngh/obs-mcp
competitors.toolSurface: Two top-level code-mode tools, search and execute, backed by 134 internal OBS operations.
competitors.rationale: Useful parity and packaging reference. Reject generated code-mode/eval/static-client architecture for Huly-style cleanroom implementation.

competitors.name: sbroenne/mcp-server-obs
competitors.runtime: C#/.NET server with TypeScript VS Code wrapper
competitors.distribution: VS Code Marketplace extension, GitHub release zip, or dotnet server execution
competitors.toolSurface: Seven action-multiplexed tools: connection, recording, streaming, scene, source, audio, media.
competitors.rationale: Strong VS Code packaging, screenshot/window-capture/virtual-camera workflows, and integration-test ideas. Avoid static singleton state and action-multiplexed schemas.

competitors.name: ironystock/agentic-obs
competitors.transport: stdio plus local HTTP dashboard/screenshot serving
competitors.toolSurface: 81 tools in 9 groups plus 4 meta-tools, resources, prompts, TUI, and web dashboard.
competitors.rationale: Best source of product/workflow ideas: capability interface, lifecycle handling, resources/prompts, screenshot retention, and stronger tests. Avoid unauthenticated HTTP exposure and non-enforced tool toggles.

competitors.name: LarsCanGit/OBS-MCP
competitors.toolSurface: 26 tools covering status, scenes, record/stream, sources, audio, canvas, scene collections, profiles, and screenshots.
competitors.rationale: Useful reference for a compact direct-control first release. Needs deeper review before it influences implementation scope.

competitors.name: takurot/obs-showrunner-mcp
competitors.license: MIT in npm metadata; no license file found during discovery
competitors.toolSurface: 18 higher-level tools for show flow, safety, audio mood, effects, overlays, snapshots, content, and scenes.
competitors.rationale: Useful for later show-director workflow tools and naming. Not a source for the core protocol layer.

competitors.name: yshk-mrt/obs-mcp
competitors.toolSurface: 23 JSON-defined OBS actions for scenes, stream/record, source visibility/transform, screenshots, media, and filters.
competitors.rationale: Useful as low-priority idea source. Do not treat as parity authority.

competitors.name: JulienCr/obs-live-suite
competitors.transport: stdio MCP plus local app services
competitors.toolSurface: 36 source-registered MCP tools in local scan; README says 26. Tools control live-production overlays, guests, posters, countdowns, chat, text presets, and overlay reload/clear.
competitors.rationale: Strong live-production workflow signal. Useful for user-facing show-production expectations, not for core obs-websocket parity.

competitors.name: skaggsxyz/moltstream
competitors.transport: MCP runtime surface plus application services
competitors.toolSurface: 9 MCP tools including get_status, start_stream, stop_stream, chat, traces, personality/config, and one obs_control multiplexer.
competitors.rationale: Strong AI-streamer product signal. Useful for chat/runtime/personality expectations, not direct OBS protocol parity.

competitors.name: rhobs/obs-mcp
competitors.toolSurface: Observability MCP tools for Prometheus, Thanos, Alertmanager, and Tempo; not OBS Studio.
competitors.rationale: Track to avoid marketplace/name confusion. Exclude from OBS Studio feature parity.

competitors.name: danielrosehill/Claude-OBS-Plugin
competitors.toolSurface: No independent OBS MCP server; setup and management workflow around Roy's package.
competitors.rationale: Track for installation, backup, and OBS setup UX. Not implementation parity.

competitors.name: @iflow-mcp/obs-mcp
competitors.rationale: Not proven independent; track as redistribution/package ecosystem signal only.

competitors.name: consigcody94/stream-pilot
competitors.toolSurface: LobeHub lists OBS+Twitch MCP with 21 tools, but source was not publicly cloneable and no npm package was found.
competitors.rationale: Revisit only if source or package becomes available.

gotchas: Use GetVersion.availableRequests for feature gating.
Vendor/custom calls are plugin/inter-client surfaces and need policy.
Scene collection switches block and can change active state.
Video settings need paired dimension and FPS validation.
Preview scene requests depend on studio mode.
Program/preview concepts do not apply to canvases.
RemoveInput removes all associated scene items.
SetInputSettings overlay defaults true.
Transition names are generally unique but not guaranteed.
T-bar APIs may be deprecated or changed later.
Filter settings overlay defaults true.
Source identity may need sourceName/sourceUuid/canvasUuid.
Generic output tools require exact outputName.
Record/stream state events are under Outputs.
Scene item IDs are per scene or group.
Source names can match multiple scene items, so searchOffset matters.
Cursor operations do not perform bounds checking.
Duration/cursor can be null when media is not playing.
Projector APIs are likely to change.
ScreenshotSaved event is for OBS hotkey screenshots, not SaveSourceScreenshot.
StopRecord returns outputPath.
Chapter markers require Hybrid MP4 as of OBS 30.2.
Stream service credentials/settings belong under Config.
Streaming integration tests require configured destinations and should be opt-in.
GetSourceScreenshot returns base64 image data.
Supported screenshot formats come from GetVersion.supportedImageFormats.
SaveSourceScreenshot needs an explicit file path policy.
v5.7 feature.
canvasUuid breaks name-only identity assumptions.

featureGaps.matrixLocation: Gaps To Add Before First Implementation Plan
featureGaps.rationale: Common recording workflow: create window capture source, list windows, bind selected window, remove source.

featureGaps.matrixLocation: Gaps To Add Before First Implementation Plan
featureGaps.rationale: Screenshots are valuable but require payload limits and filesystem/path policy.

featureGaps.matrixLocation: Gaps To Add Before First Implementation Plan
featureGaps.rationale: Common OBS control surface after stream/record are stable.

featureGaps.matrixLocation: Gaps To Add Before First Implementation Plan
featureGaps.rationale: Useful for automation and state resources, but needs bounded buffers and clear session lifecycle.

featureGaps.matrixLocation: Gaps To Add Before First Implementation Plan
featureGaps.rationale: Useful for animation and multi-step workflows, but error ordering and halt behavior should be modeled after core request correctness.
In-process code execution conflicts with the Huly-style explicit typed tool registry and safety posture.
